name: Masquerade Elden Ring boot-init load behind a clean custom title cover

description: baseline current spike/boot-parallelization before title-cover masquerade changes

asi.hypothesis: Baseline establishes existing static/autoload proof score before implementing Part A title MenuWindowJob suppression or Part B custom cover injection.
asi.task_direction: Masquerade irreducible boot-init behind intentional title/loading visual; no ms target; validate A disable-native-title-view and B custom cover separately.
hard_constraints: do not touch STEP_Wait or CSMenuMan+0x21

description: attempted Part A BeginTitle native-title visual suppression but static measure failed to see constants/source anchors

asi.hypothesis: Suppress only the BeginTitle 05_000_Title MenuWindowJob wrapper and expose telemetry; extend the score so Part A and Part B are independently penalized.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary score did not improve because .auto/measure.sh checked constants in a source string that did not include src/constants.rs, so Part A was still reported missing; Part B remains missing by design.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix measure to include constants, then reapply the Part A hook/telemetry/checker changes and rerun. Keep the two-cover-failure scoring: old baseline remains comparable, Part A should reduce title_cover_failures from 2 to 1.
details: Ghidra decompile showed FUN_14081f9f0 only constructs CSScaleformLoadInfo filename 05_000_Title and calls FUN_1407acbf0.
Implementation shape used a MinHook detour returning param_1 after writing null to *out_slot, matching factory allocation-failure shape.
Need rerun static checker after reapply; check-autoload-happy-path had passed before measure failure.

description: Part A suppresses native 05_000_Title BeginTitle visual wrapper and exposes telemetry

asi.hypothesis: Part A can hide the native title view safely by detouring only BeginTitle's 05_000_Title wrapper (0x14081f9f0) and returning a null MenuWindowJob out slot, leaving TitleStep/STEP_Wait/native Continue untouched.
asi.evidence: Static Ghidra: dump 0x14081fae0 (deobf 0x14081f9f0) builds CSScaleformLoadInfo filename 05_000_Title then calls factory 0x1407acbf0; deobf/dump shift +0xf0 verified by scripts/dump-deobf-shift.py --reverse.
asi.score_reason: title_cover_failures reduced from 2 to 1; remaining title_cover failure is Part B custom cover render path not yet implemented.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement Part B custom image render path or resolve asset blocker: inspect 05_001_Title_Logo for dummy texture remap; otherwise author a small Scaleform target referencing MENU_DummyProfileFace_NN/SYSTEX_Menu_ProfileNN. Then runtime-validate A and B separately.

description: Resolved Part B asset fork: 05_001_Title_Logo has no dummy-profile/SYSTEX texture symbol

asi.hypothesis: Part B should stop targeting 05_001_Title_Logo directly if its extracted GFX has no MENU_DummyProfileFace/SYSTEX symbol; a custom Scaleform target or different dummy-profile surface is required.
asi.evidence: Offline asset strings from /home/banon/er-extract/nuxe-menu-20260619-170932/menu/05_001_title_logo.gfx list static title textures (MENU_Title_GR, MENU_Title_EldenRing, MENU_DS3_LOGO, MENU_Title_EldenRing_01) and no MENU_DummyProfileFace or SYSTEX_Menu_Profile. Control 05_000_title.gfx also lacks profile dummy symbols.
asi.score_reason: Part B asset-decision penalty cleared; remaining title_cover_penalty=50 is the actual custom cover render implementation/proof.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement/render a custom cover target referencing MENU_DummyProfileFace_NN / SYSTEX_Menu_ProfileNN, or find an existing Scaleform surface with those symbols; do not keep trying 05_001_Title_Logo remap.

description: Part B static cover path replaces 05_000_Title with ProfileSelect dummy-profile/SYSTEX surface

asi.hypothesis: Instead of authoring a new GFX immediately, reuse the existing ProfileSelect Scaleform as the cover because it already contains MENU_DummyProfileFace_01..10 placeholders mapped to SYSTEX_Menu_Profile targets.
asi.evidence: Ghidra xrefs: 05_010_ProfileSelect wrappers at dump 0x14081f6f0/0x14081f7e0; dump-deobf-shift maps chosen wrapper to deobf 0x14081f6f0. Extracted 05_010_profileselect.gfx contains MENU_DummyProfileFace_01..10. The BeginTitle detour now clears the 05_000 out slot then calls the 05_010 ProfileSelect wrapper and records oracle_title_custom_cover_profile_select_* telemetry.
asi.score_reason: Static title_cover_penalty reached 0; remaining score loss is pre-existing readiness/runtime-proof debt, not the cover static path.
asi.runtime_caveat: This is not runtime proof yet. Next run must prove no crash, native 05_000 gone, ProfileSelect/dummy cover visible, zero MessageBoxDialog, zero input, and gold loads (Banon).
asi.next_action_hint: Run the bounded product runtime probe only after checking Steam is running; validate A and B separately from telemetry plus teardown screenshot. If ProfileSelect cover conflicts with title flow, fallback is a minimal custom Scaleform target using the same dummy-profile symbol.

description: runtime probe with ProfileSelect cover built A/B but timed out before gold load

asi.hypothesis: Runtime should prove the ProfileSelect cover replacement is safe: native 05_000_Title suppressed, custom ProfileSelect cover built, zero input, no MessageBoxDialog, and Banon loads behind it.
asi.error_details: run_experiment timed out; artifact target/runtime-probe/product-continue-direct-20260624-184915 reached first telemetry at +2.3s and watcher teardown at +40.1s with reason=timeout_seconds_budget_exhausted. Telemetry had oracle_title_native_menu_visual_suppressed_builds=1 and oracle_title_custom_cover_profile_select_builds=1, simulated_button_presses_total=0, oracle_msgbox_total_builds=0, but oracle_char_name='' and oracle_player_present=false.
asi.rollback_reason: Runtime did not prove gold load/world-stable. Treat as failed runtime experiment even though A/B cover telemetry worked.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate why replacing the BeginTitle visual with ProfileSelect leaves owner_state=10 / no Continue progress. Likely the ProfileSelect visual job perturbs the title menu's press/start or MenuJobWait pump; next experiment should preserve Part A null suppression for load and render cover through a separate surface/overlay path rather than replacing the title job slot, or make the replacement job passive/non-blocking.

description: attempted to revert ProfileSelect cover replacement back to Part A null suppression but existing measure still credited stale Part B tokens

asi.hypothesis: After runtime showed ProfileSelect replacement blocks title progress, revert the BeginTitle hook to pure null suppression so Part A can be runtime-validated independently and leave Part B as unresolved.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was unchanged because .auto/measure.sh still credited stale TITLE_CUSTOM_COVER/oracle tokens after the hook no longer built the custom cover. The experiment did not produce a trustworthy metric improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: First harden .auto/measure.sh so Part B requires an actual observable cover build path (e.g. TITLE_CUSTOM_COVER_PROFILE_SELECT_BUILDS.fetch_add in the hook or a successor runtime observable). Then reapply the PartA-only null suppression change and run the static measure/runtime probe.

description: Part A-only null suppression makes measure honestly mark Part B still missing

asi.hypothesis: Remove the product-unsafe ProfileSelect job replacement and let the metric honestly score Part B as still missing, so Part A null suppression can later be validated separately at runtime.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric decreased from the kept 1430 to 1380 because Part B is intentionally missing. This change is not independently keepable without a successful runtime proof that removes runtime_proof_failures or a non-blocking custom cover implementation.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the PartA-only null suppression change only as part of a runtime-validation experiment; use .auto/run_runtime_probe_once so a successful gold load can offset the PartB penalty. If runtime succeeds, keep that as validated Part A and continue Part B with a non-blocking/custom surface.
